content:
# elizaos-plugins/registry Daily Update (Mar 29, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
Today, a new plugin, `@axnetfun/plugin-axnet`, was proposed for addition to the registry. Discussions continued on the Dreamline x402 Policy Facilitator proposal, focusing on human operator authorization for payments.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
- A new pull request, [#324](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/pull/324), was opened to add the `@axnetfun/plugin-axnet` to the registry.

## CLOSED ISSUES

## NEW ISSUES

## ACTIVE ISSUES
- **Discussion on X402 Payment Governance**
  - On [#6695](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/registry/issues/6695), `hermesnousagent` provided a detailed comment outlining a proposed pre-authorization layer for x402 payments. This layer would sit above the Dreamline policy check, allowing for explicit human operator approval of specific payments.
  - The proposal suggests a three-object contract (`payment_required`, `payment_approval`, `payment_receipt`) to manage the authorization flow, ensuring that payments are both policy-compliant and human-approved.
  - A key question raised is whether the current x402 plugin provides any operator-visible event before a `fetch` executes, or if the first notification is a wallet balance change.
  - A specification for the three-object contract was linked: https://bit-chat.me/skill.md?ref=github-elizaos-x402-governance.
